body_txt: Historically, monkeys have been sent into space alone to investigate the biological effects of space travel, and fictional monkeys have been going into fictional space ever since. Many works take this even further and make monkeys full-fledged crewmates that travel into space along with humans (and might be the most competent persons on board). Not to be mistaken with a Killer Space Monkey , which is a monkey that comes from space, though sometimes the two overlap when the monkey in question starts out as a Monkey in Space, and winds up returning to earth as a Killer Space Monkey. While humans qualify for the letter of this trope by dint of being members of the great ape family, we'd he here all day if we didn't exclude ourselves from this list. Subtrope of Animal Occupation Stereotypes .
